rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 50 'Practice beats external proof.' uses practice(n); permitted under the AE101 carve-out — earned at M4, fluent M5-M6. This is an M5 file. No ritual/ceremony in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body sits in the AE101 quintet (Boris-direct platform truth + Martin frame-move on test/learn/encode). M5 long-running mood; Risto does not lead. No atmospheric imagery, no negation-callout self-contradiction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Articles-only rule; target is a lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Three failure modes and /compact-at-60% are convergent practitioner vocabulary (no single originator); Ronacher names deferred to closer by design (maintainer note). No creator-name (Antti) or author-we in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses 'you' throughout (lines 3-52). Delivery mechanics ('Land it slowly. Pause after.') live below the maintainer cut (line 79).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: test->learn->encode is unfolded plainly in body (line 11). Three lenses named operationally (lines 17-21). No bare maintainer term-of-art (three-pattern, Ronacher names) leaks above the cut.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Hands-off FAQ presents two options factually ('Sometimes useful, sometimes wrong. Better than dropping context entirely.') without reassurance-shaped 'it's OK if / no penalty' apology.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-artefact authoring rule; not a lecture-body lint.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No named practitioner is cited in body at all; attribution lives in pre-read and maintainer block. No over-crediting.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Slogan 'Diagnose first. Fix later.' (line 7) is immediately bounded by the paragraph below it (line 9) naming why and when. 'Practice beats external proof.' (line 50) lands its carve-out in the same line ('The run shows you what no benchmark can.').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No vendor/marketing-defense phrasing in body. 'The run shows you what no benchmark can.' (line 50) is a teaching claim, not a positioning defense. No 'vendor' token in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Operational terms (/compact, /context, auto-compact, ccstatusline) are M5 session-management moves taught here; they land operationally in this same lecture and the M5 exercise. No LLM-internal vocabulary planted ahead of arc.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Closer 'Open your repo. Let's go.' (line 52) is a clean session-start cue. No dangling demonstratives, no bypass-the-agent punchline ('write that yourself').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture opener, not a post-action recap. 'What is about to happen' (line 42) is correctly forward-tense; no 'What happens/happened' recap section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Student->agent verbs stay peer-flat ('ask it to read', 'ask what validation', line 44-47). No bark/order/command/whip.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'the reflex is to fix it immediately' (line 9) names a universal failure mode, not 'you're missing Y'. No ability-tier pegging. Framings hold at every level.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Bare MN (M4/M5/M6) fine — M5 file in arc. Three lenses each get a one-sentence primer (lines 17-21). /compact earned with its operational why (line 27).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Agents 101 business-audience default; this is an AE101 software-engineer-IC file where earned tech terms are expected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'context' used precisely as runtime state (lines 26-38: 'context fills', 'choose what to keep', 'auto-compact can fire and the agent can re-anchor from what survives'). 'something durable to re-read' is the persistent-artifact side. No 'your context compounds' blur.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Hands-off FAQ (lines 32-41) presents two forward design choices for the re-send, not troubleshooting/recovery branches. Fallback note for moved repo lives below maintainer cut (line 69).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt-structure rule; this opener contains no fenced student prompt with output destination.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Debrief/compound-shape rule; this is a lecture opener with no Debrief.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice is M5 diagnostic/long-running mood throughout; no M1 joyful-creation register borrowed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase 1 reads the M4 artefact through three diagnostic lenses (line 44) for learning, not as manual error-catching of Claude's live output. Phase 2 routes catching into validation, not manual diff.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Self-study simulation-design rule; not applicable to an in-room lecture body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Practitioner sourcing stays inline ('What works, per recent practitioner posts', line 27). No research callout box.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forcing function is in-chat (ask the session to read/quote, ask what validation would catch). /compact is a native in-conversation slash command, not editor/terminal mechanic.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No CLAUDE.md reference in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No em-dashes in body. Sentences are short and declarative. Parentheticals (lines 27, 37) are true asides.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture section headers are declarative noun-phrases ('Three lenses', 'One operational move', 'What is about to happen') — permitted; reader's job is understanding, not doing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Only link is '[ccstatusline](https://github.com/sirmalloc/ccstatusline)' (line 27) — link text is the tool's name, not a filename path. External URL, not a curriculum surface.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No student-authored write to a contested word here (no 'memory'/'skill'/'rule' file the student authors in this opener). The opener stages Phase 1-4; authoring happens in the exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'the agent' used for autonomous acting (lines 9, 17-20, 38); 'the model' for the deciding system (lines 27, 37); 'Claude Code session' for the product surface (line 44). Agent-count is healthy; not assistant-shaped.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Today is the learn' (line 11) is session-relative ('today' as this-session), not a time-of-day anchor (tomorrow/overnight/this morning). 'will run for hours while you're away' (line 33) is a duration mechanic, preserved. No banned time-of-day token.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No pedagogy-aware headers or contemplative-pause prefixes in body. 'Don't generalise. Quote.' (line 44) is a direct command, not 'sit with' / 'let it land'. The 'Land it slowly. Pause after.' delivery note is below the cut (line 79).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No Claude-asks-student-what-they-decided meta-loop. Phase prompts have the student ask the agent to read/quote/propose; student steers.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'save it as <filename>' prompt in this opener body; no artifact filename to default.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Every beat advances the M5 stance (diagnose-first, three lenses, one operational move, hands-off FAQ, phase map). No implementation trivia or maintainer reasoning leaking into body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'read it'/'look at it'/'notice X' after a chat-print. 'you have something to read now' (line 3) refers to the M4 artefact existing, not narrating a chat-print read.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forward callouts keep 'you' as subject ('you won't be there', line 33; 'you can leave the room', line 41). No training-as-subject, no coach-imperative, no role-gating to a different-you.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The one operational setting this opener depends on (/compact at ~60%) is restated at moment of use (line 27) with its rationale, not assumed carried from prework.
